scope.summary: Implement the Free Sewing authoring operation exposing partial-edge sub-range [0,1] and numeric seam-length override on SeamEndpoint via the model-first API and CRDT edit surface
acceptance_criteria: SeamEndpoint::range: Option<[f32; 2]> is validated to [0.0,1.0] before sandbox invocation; out-of-range values produce a SEAM_EDGE_REF blocking error with field_path
The author_garment MCP tool accepts SeamSpec with partial range values on one or both endpoints and produces correctly resampled SeamConstraintRecord entries proportional to the sub-range arc length

A numeric seam-length override (length_cm override on SeamEndpoint) is supported so the model can set exact gather lengths independent of edge geometry; the override is stored in the SeamSpec and validated against edge length bounds
Partial-edge seams survive GarmentSpec round-trip to/from GarmentCode JSON without losing range values
SEAM_LENGTH_MATCH ValidationDescriptor (MT-134) confirms partial-edge seam pairs match within gather tolerance at run end
proof_targets: Applies to HBR-INT: author_garment tool call with partial-range SeamSpec -> EventLedger TailorGarmentDraftProposed -> sandbox sim -> SEAM_LENGTH_MATCH passes
scope.risk_if_missed: Garments requiring gathered necklines, yoke inserts, sleeve caps, and any seam where edge lengths differ require Free Sewing. Without it, every panel must be pre-split at exact vertices, making gathered garment authoring impractical for models and operators. Sleeve cap ease (the critical shaping seam of any fitted top) cannot be expressed.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
